title: T-114 — UI: CostPanel (material + labour + overhead + margin) (NPD-g)
prompt:
# T-114 — CostPanel slice

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md §17.11.1
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/recipe.jsx:67-101 (CostPanel)
Prototype-index entry: cost_panel

## Goal
Single-component slice for the live cost breakdown panel. Displays raw material, after-yield, processing, packaging, and total cost per kg; revenue/kg, margin €/kg, margin %; plus target-price and yield % inputs that propagate to parent state. All values currency-formatted and reactive to ingredient changes.

## Implementation contract
- apps/web/app/(npd)/formulations/[id]/editor/_components/cost-panel.tsx ('use client'). Props: { calc: CostBreakdown, targetPrice: string, onTargetPriceChange: (v: string) => void, yieldPct: number, onYieldChange: (n: number) => void }.
- Use shadcn Card + Input + Label per prototype_index translation_notes. Cost lines use Tailwind flex justify-between with monospace numerics.
- Currency format via Intl.NumberFormat ('en-GB', currency: 'EUR') for production; expose currency from project context in wiring task (T-117) — for this slice accept currency prop with default 'EUR'.
- Margin box background tinted red/amber/green by marginPct thresholds (< 0, < 15, ≥ 15) per prototype.

## Files (≤ 3)
- apps/web/app/(npd)/formulations/[id]/editor/_components/cost-panel.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(npd)/formulations/[id]/editor/_components/__tests__/cost-panel.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the panel renders with a fixed calc prop, when compared to prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/recipe.jsx:67-101 (CostPanel), then it shows raw material / after-yield / processing / packaging / total cost lines plus margin box with revenue, €/kg margin and margin % — verified by RTL parity
2. Given the user types in the target-price or yield input, when onChange fires, then the controlled value reaches the parent via onTargetPriceChange/onYieldChange callbacks — verified by RTL fireEvent + spy
3. Given calc.marginPct is < 0, < 15, or ≥ 15, when rendered, then the margin box uses red / amber / green tint respectively per prototype thresholds — verified by RTL class assertion
4. Given any monetary value is rendered, then it is currency-formatted (e.g. '€3.98') — verified by RTL text-match

## Test strategy
- RED: cost-panel.test.tsx asserting structural parity, controlled-input callbacks, margin-tint thresholds, currency formatting — before component exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter web test cost-panel'

## Risk red lines
- Do not hardcode currency symbol — accept from prop with default 'EUR'
- Do not own targetPrice/yieldPct state inside the panel (controlled component)
- Do not paste prototype JSX verbatim
